_comment: PEX 03 — Drone Delivery Mission — Configuration File
Edit the values in this file to configure your mission without
changing any Python source code.  Every tunable parameter in the
Any key starting with '_' is ignored by the loader, so you can
add as many _comment blocks as you like for documentation.
[ ] connect_string      = correct port for your hardware
[ ] use_estimated_distance confirmed for your hardware setup
_comment_connection: connect_string : MAVLink connection string passed to DroneKit.
The format depends on how the companion computer is connected
to the flight controller:
USB autopilot   → '/dev/ttyACM0'
UART serial     → '/dev/ttyS0'
This is the most common value to change between bench testing
(SITL) and real hardware deployment.
baud : Serial baud rate for USB / UART connections.
The standard ArduPilot rate is 115200.
Ignored for TCP/UDP connections.
virtual_mode : Set to true when running in the SITL simulator.
When true:
- The rangefinder check is skipped at startup
- deliver_package() lands in place instead of lowering a tether
- Camera windows are displayed on screen via cv2.imshow()
- get_avg_distance_to_obj() returns a fixed simulated value
Set to false for every real hardware flight.
⚠  VERIFY this is false before every live flight.
takeoff_altitude_m : Altitude in metres the drone climbs to after
arming before the AUTO waypoint mission begins.
~15 m (≈ 45 ft) is a good starting point for open-area testing.
_comment_paths: weights_path : Absolute path to the YOLOv8 TensorRT engine file
(or a .pt weights file for non-TensorRT inference).
This is the detection model used by both check_for_initial_target()
Update this path to match where your team has placed the model.
mission_log_path : Directory where mission frame snapshots (.png)
and the timestamped log file are written during flight.
The directory is created automatically if it does not exist.
TODO: Change 'cam' to your team name so runs don't overwrite
_comment_camera_hardware: These fields describe the physical RealSense camera installed on
the drone.  The application reads these at startup to configure
the camera pipeline — nothing is hardcoded to any specific model.
When you swap to a different camera, update these values.
camera_name : Human-readable label used only in log messages.
No functional effect.  Example: 'Intel RealSense D455'.
camera_has_imu : Whether the camera has a built-in IMU
(accelerometer + gyroscope).
true  → D455 has a built-in IMU.  Automatic tilt measurement
and IMU-based distance estimation are available.
false → D435 / D415 do NOT have IMUs.  Set camera_tilt_deg
manually and camera_tilt_from_imu will be ignored.
camera_resolution_width / height : Color stream resolution in pixels.
Must match a supported mode for your camera model.
Higher resolutions improve detection at range but increase
CPU load.  640×480 is recommended for Raspberry Pi 4.
camera_frame_rate : Color stream frame rate (fps).
30 fps is the safe starting point.  60 fps is achievable
at 640×480 on a D455 over USB 3.0.
camera_imu_accel_rate : Accelerometer sample rate in Hz.
Only used when camera_has_imu is true.
D455 supports 63 Hz and 200 Hz.  200 Hz is recommended.
camera_imu_gyro_rate : Gyroscope sample rate in Hz.
Only used when camera_has_imu is true.
D455 supports 200 Hz and 400 Hz.  400 Hz is recommended.
camera_fov_h_deg : Horizontal field-of-view of the Color sensor (degrees).
Used in IMU roll compensation to convert drone roll angle to
false pixel shift.  Do NOT change unless you have swapped the lens.
D455 stock lens ≈ 87°
D435 stock lens ≈ 87°
D415 stock lens ≈ 65°
camera_fov_v_deg : Vertical field-of-view of the Color sensor (degrees).
Required for estimate_ground_distance_m() — the formula that
computes ground distance from pixel row, altitude, and camera tilt.
D455 stock lens ≈ 58°.  Do not change unless the lens is swapped.
_comment_camera_geometry: These parameters describe how the camera is physically mounted
on the drone.  They affect both distance estimation and the
IMU roll compensation calculation.
flip_vertical : Set to true when the RealSense is mounted upside-down.
Flips the image top-to-bottom before any processing occurs.
If the video looks correct but appears upside-down, enable this.
camera_tilt_deg : Angle the camera points BELOW horizontal (degrees).
Measure from your physical airframe mount.
0   = perfectly horizontal (forward-facing)
37.5 = midpoint of the typical 30–45° downward range
90  = straight down
This value is used as a FALLBACK only when camera_tilt_from_imu
is false or when the IMU tilt measurement fails at startup.
It directly affects estimate_ground_distance_m() accuracy —
a 2° error in tilt produces noticeable range errors at altitude.
camera_tilt_from_imu : When true, the application measures the actual
camera tilt from the IMU before arming (while the drone is sitting
still on level ground).  The measured value replaces camera_tilt_deg
for all calculations.  Requires camera_has_imu = true.
If the IMU is unavailable, falls back silently to camera_tilt_deg.
Strongly recommended when camera_has_imu is true — eliminates the
need to manually measure and update camera_tilt_deg.
camera_tilt_sample_duration_s : How many seconds to sample the IMU
when measuring camera tilt at startup.  Longer durations give
more stable readings but delay the mission start.
2.0 seconds is usually sufficient.  Increase to 3–4 s in
windy conditions or if the drone is on uneven ground.
_comment_imu: The camera's built-in IMU is used to:
1. Measure the camera's actual downward tilt at startup
(see camera_tilt_from_imu above).
2. Estimate ground distance to the target using camera geometry
when use_estimated_distance is true.
imu_enabled : Master switch for the IMU reader thread.
When false, both tilt measurement and estimated distance fall
back to configured/manual values.  Automatically forced false
if camera_has_imu is false.
If IMU streams are unavailable at runtime (USB 2.0 bandwidth,
missing firmware, etc.) the system falls back gracefully —
it will not crash.
imu_complementary_alpha : Gyroscope weight in the complementary
filter that blends gyro integration (fast, drifts over time)
with accelerometer tilt (slow, drift-free).
angle = alpha × (angle + gyro_rate × dt)
+ (1 − alpha) × accel_angle
Higher (→ 1.0) = faster response, slower drift correction.
Lower  (→ 0.0) = slower response, faster drift correction.
0.98 is the standard starting value.  Do not go below 0.90.
_comment_distance: These settings control how the drone estimates the ground distance
to the target during the DELIVER state — needed to calculate the
precise GPS drop coordinates.
use_estimated_distance : When true, ground distance is calculated
from camera geometry using estimate_ground_distance_m().
This uses the target's pixel row in the frame, the camera's
vertical FOV, the measured tilt angle, and the drone's GPS
altitude to estimate how far away the target is on the ground.
No rangefinder hardware is required.
Assumptions:
- Ground below the target is approximately flat.
- camera_tilt_deg (or IMU-measured tilt) is accurate.
- GPS altitude is reasonably accurate (barometric drift < 1 m).
When false, the rangefinder sensor is used instead.
Use true if your drone does not have a rangefinder, or as a
fallback when the rangefinder returns invalid readings.
Geometry (for reference):
depression_angle = camera_tilt + pixel_row_offset_from_center
× vertical_fov
ground_distance  = altitude / tan(depression_angle)
_comment_detection: These parameters control the YOLOv8 detection and histogram tracking
pipeline in object_tracking_y8_histo.py.
detect_confidence : Minimum YOLO confidence score (0.0–1.0) used
during check_for_initial_target() (SEEK state) and during
track_with_confirm() (TARGET state).
Lower values increase sensitivity but raise false positives.
0.25 is a good starting point for the VisDrone model at
typical mission altitudes.
detect_every_n : In SEEK mode, run the YOLO detection scan every
N frames.  1 = scan every frame (maximum sensitivity, higher
CPU load).  Increase to 2 or 3 on slower hardware.
model_imgsz : YOLO inference resolution (pixels, square).
Must match the resolution the engine was exported at if using
a static TensorRT engine.  640 matches the native camera
stream resolution and avoids a resize step.
tracker_misses_max : Consecutive frames with no histogram match
above histo_match_threshold before the target is declared lost
and the mission returns to SEEK.  At 10 FPS, 20 ≈ 2 seconds.
Increase if the target is frequently lost due to brief occlusion.
Decrease if you want faster recovery from a false lock.
histo_match_threshold : Minimum histogram correlation score (0.0–1.0)
for a detection to be accepted as the tracked target.
Range 0.0 (no similarity) to 1.0 (identical).
0.70 is permissive enough to handle lighting variation and
partial occlusion without being so strict that the target is
frequently dropped.
Raise (→ 0.85) in crowded scenes to reduce false swaps.
Lower (→ 0.55) if the target is frequently lost due to
changing lighting or the person rotating away from the camera.
_comment_mission: These parameters tune the mission state machine in pex03.py.
max_confirm_attempts : How many times the drone will try to re-acquire
a candidate target in CONFIRM mode before giving up and returning
to SEEK.  Each attempt involves flying back to the sighting location
and rotating to a new angle.  Higher values give more chances to
confirm a real target but delay resuming the waypoint mission if
the initial detection was a false positive.
target_acceptance_radius_px : Radius in pixels of the 'centred'
acceptance zone drawn on the camera frame.  When the target's
centre pixel falls within this radius of the frame centre, the
drone is considered directly above the target and transitions
from TARGET mode to DELIVER mode.
Smaller = more precise positioning required before delivery.
Larger  = delivery triggers sooner, less accurate drop.
10 px at 640×480 resolution is a reasonable starting value.
update_rate : The SEEK detection scan runs every N frames.
1 = run every frame.  Higher values reduce CPU load during the
scanning phase but decrease the chance of catching a briefly
visible target.
image_log_rate : Save an annotated snapshot to mission_log_path
every N frames.  4 = save every 4th frame.  Increase to reduce
disk usage on long flights; decrease for more detailed logs.
